# Third-Party Bundled Tools

NGIN can use bundled third-party build backend tools from `Tools/ThirdParty/BuildTools` when they are present. The tool payload directories are generated by the fetch scripts and are intentionally kept separate from the authored manifest and notices.

## CMake

- Version: 4.3.2
- License: BSD-3-Clause
- Homepage: https://cmake.org/
- Source and binary distribution: https://github.com/Kitware/CMake/releases/tag/v4.3.2

Redistribution must preserve the CMake license text and the license material for third-party components included in the CMake distribution. Keep `LICENSE.rst` and the `Licenses/` directory from the official CMake distribution with every bundled CMake payload.

## Ninja

- Version: 1.13.2
- License: Apache-2.0
- Homepage: https://ninja-build.org/
- Source and binary distribution: https://github.com/ninja-build/ninja/releases/tag/v1.13.2

Redistribution must preserve Ninja's `COPYING` file. If a future Ninja distribution includes additional notice material, keep it with the bundled payload as well.

## Update Rules

- Use official upstream release artifacts unless there is a documented reason to self-build.
- Update `Tools/ThirdParty/BuildTools/toolchains.json` when changing versions or URLs.
- Preserve upstream license and notice files inside each extracted payload.
- Record checksums for downloaded artifacts before publishing a release bundle.
- Do not use upstream project names or marks in a way that implies endorsement.
